title: Kansas City Crime, 1985–2026 — Halved From the 1991 Peak, Across 240 Neighborhoods
titleOptions: Kansas City Crime Was Cut in Half — 40 Years of Data Across 240 Neighborhoods
From 58,374 to 28,885 — How Kansas City Crime Halved (Then the Count Changed)
description:
Forty years of reported crime in Kansas City, Missouri — a 51% fall from the 1991 peak to 2014 under the FBI's measure, then the city's own report-level record across 240 neighborhoods.

This is a fully sourced, reproducible data visualization. Every figure comes straight from the FBI Uniform Crime Reporting program and KCPD's own open crime data. Every dot on the map is a real reported incident location at block level, as geocoded by KCPD. Coverage and caveats are shown on screen — 86.8% of reports map to a named neighborhood; the rest are counted and disclosed, never invented.

The FBI's classic 'index' crimes (through 2014) and KCPD's deduplicated reports (2015+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, never merged into one line. KCPD publishes one row per involvement; every count here is deduplicated to one per report.

▶ CHAPTERS
0:00  Hook — halved
0:08  What you're about to see
0:22  Chapter 1 — the long arc
2:30  The last five years — up close
2:43  Chapter 2 — the map, month by month
4:52  The busiest & safest areas
5:18  Sources & credits

▶ MADE BY AN AI — FULLY TRANSPARENT
This video was produced end-to-end by Claude (Anthropic's AI): it located and fetched the official source data, built and validated the data pipeline, verified every on-screen figure against the sources, designed and rendered the visuals, and generated the music. No human edited the numbers. Every step — code, data, and design decisions — is public and reproducible in the repository linked below. If you find an error, open an issue; corrections are part of the record.

▶ CODE, DATA & FULL SOURCES (reproduce every number)
https://github.com/hadi-nayebi/crime-cartography/tree/main/videos/kansas-city-mo

Data: Kansas City, Missouri Police Department via data.kcmo.org (public domain where stated; 2015–2017 assets carry no license field, attributed to KCPD). Music generated with Stable Audio Open (Stability AI). Basemap © OpenStreetMap contributors.

If this was worth a look, please Like, Subscribe, and Share.
